moduleTitle: Identification, assessment, and management of dependencies, impacts, risks, and opportunities
questionText: How does your organization define substantive effects on your organization?
questionKo: 귀사가 정의하는 중대한 영향 (중대한 영향) 의 기준을 설명하십시오
issueTags: Water, Ocean, Biodiversity, Plastics, Climate Change, Forests
sectorTags: Financial services, Oil & Gas, Coal, General, Aviation, Capital goods, Transport OEMS - EPM, Chemicals, Metals & mining, Transport services, Steel, Agricultural commodities, Transport OEMS, Energy utilities & power generators, Biodiversity - Mines, Cement, Food, beverage & tobacco, Paper & forestry, Real estate, Construction
simpleHelp: Indicators and thresholds employed to define which effects on the organization are considered substantive can differ among disclosers depending on their sector, value chain, or geography. Therefore, outlining clear definitions on how organizations determine potential effects as substantive provides critical context to data users. This question increases transparency and improves clarity as to what the discloser deems to be a substantive effect on their organization.

columns.text: Indicator used to define substantive effect

options: Time horizon over which the effect occurs

guidance.text:
General 
This question seeks to define and quantify how your organization defines a ‘substantive effect' on your organization at the corporate level, in the context of environmental risks and opportunities. This will be used to understand the threshold for your reported risks and opportunities in 3.1 and 3.6. 
What constitutes a substantive effect will vary between organizations. You are therefore asked to determine ‘substantive' in the way that you would do for your business decision-making. For example, a 1% reduction in profits will have different effects on different organizations depending on its respective profit margins. A substantive effect of relatively high magnitude could occur because of a large impact on one of the following aspects, or because of a small number for all three combines to create a larger effect: 
the proportion of business units affected; 
the size of the effect on those business units; 
the dependency of the organization on that unit; and/or 
the potential for shareholder or customer concern. 
Use the "Add row" function if you define a substantive effect on your organization with respect to risks or opportunities in more than one way. For example, if you use multiple indicators or have different definitions for different parts of the business. 
% Change to indicator (column 5) 
This column only appears if "Quantitative" is selected in column 2 "Type of definition" and either "% increase" or "% decrease" is selected in column 4 "Change to indicator".
Metrics considered in definition (column 7) 
This column seeks to capture the different factors taken into account when defining a substantive effect on your organization with respect to risks and opportunities. 
If you have provided a quantitative definition, these metrics could give further context to your quantitative indicator. For example, you may define risks to be substantive if they could lead to a 10% decrease in revenue over your short-term time horizon (as defined in 2.1). In this case, select "Time horizon over which the effect occurs" and provide further details in column 8 "Application of definition". 
If you are only providing a qualitative definition, select all relevant metrics used to define a substantive effect, or use "Other, please specify" to specify a different metric. 
Application of definition (column 8) 
Describe the thresholds considered to be substantive for the metrics reported in column 7 "Metrics considered in definition". For example: 
Frequency of effect occurring: i.e. once, quarterly, every two years etc. 
Time horizon over which the effect occurs: i.e., short-, medium-, and long-term in line with your time horizons reported in 2.1. 
Likelihood of effect occurring: i.e., the percentage chance the effect will occur 
Often, multiple metrics are used together as part of a matrix approach. If using a matrix approach, describe how the metrics selected in column 7 "Metrics considered in definition" are used in combination and their respective weightings. 
Describe how often the metrics, and their thresholds, are selected, reviewed, and updated.
guidance.textKo: 이 섹션은 해당 문항에서 CDP가 요청하는 작성 내용을 설명합니다. 위 응답방법, 열 구조, 드롭다운 선택지를 기준으로 보고연도와 조직 범위에 맞는 정보를 입력하십시오.

guidance.text:
With respect to the identification, assessment, and management of substantive environmental dependencies, impacts, risks, and opportunities, the organization:
Describes how the organization defines substantive effects.
guidance.textKo: 이 섹션은 문항 응답에 필요한 추가 작성 기준을 설명합니다. 응답방법과 선택지를 함께 확인하여 작성하십시오.

guidance.text: Substantive effect : an effect that has a considerable or relatively significant effect on an organization at the corporate level in terms of risks and opportunities. This could include operational, financial, or strategic effects that undermine, or provide opportunities for, the entire organization or part of the organization.
guidance.textKo: 이 섹션은 문항에서 사용하는 주요 용어의 의미를 설명합니다. 용어 해석은 CDP 2026 원문과 국문 양식의 용어 사용을 우선 적용하십시오.

criteria.criteria:
Points will be awarded per completed cell in proportion to the number of rows disclosed. Partially completed rows will not receive full points.
A maximum of 6/6 points is available for this question.
criteria.criteriaKo: 점 부여됨 마다 완료 셀 비례 숫자 입력 의 행 공시된. 부분적으로 완료된 행은 최고점을 받을 수 없습니다. 이 문항에서 최대 6/6점을 받을 수 있습니다.

criteria.criteria:
At least one row where 'Risks' is selected in column 'Effect type' completed - 1 point
A maximum of 1/1 point is available for this question.
One row scored.
criteria.criteriaKo: 에서 최소 1개 행 경우 '리스크' 열에서 선택 '영향 유형' 완료 - 1 점 이 문항에서 최대 1/1점을 받을 수 있습니다. 1개 행 평가.

criteria.criteria:
All of the following is met - 1 point
- 'Quantitative' selected in column 'Type of definition' in any complete row where 'Risks' selected in column 'Effect type'
- 'Qualitative' selected in column 'Type of definition' in any complete row where 'Risks' selected in column 'Effect type'
- 'Quantitative' selected in column 'Type of definition' in any complete row where 'Opportunities' selected in column 'Effect type'
- 'Qualitative' selected in column 'Type of definition' in any complete row where 'Opportunities' selected in column 'Effect type'
Best row(s) scored.
A maximum of 1/1 point is available for this question.

change.question_ko: 귀사가 정의하는 중대한 영향 (중대한 영향) 의 기준을 설명하십시오
change.q2025_text: How does your organization define substantive effects on your organization?
change.scoring_change_summary: CC/F/W All sectors Management • Scoring criteria modified to allow organizations to report ‘Quantitative' and ‘Qualitative' definitions of substantive risks and opportunities in separate rows. Scoring adapted to account for organizations for whom it may be more applicable to report quantitative and qualitative definitions in separate rows.
change.reflected:
2026 question text applied
2026 response type/columns applied
2026 D/A/M/L point allocation linked
2026 scoring criteria linked
change.scoring_change_summaryKo: 이 섹션은 문항 응답에 필요한 추가 작성 기준을 설명합니다. 응답방법과 선택지를 함께 확인하여 작성하십시오.
change.q2025_textKo: 귀사가 정의하는 중대한 영향 (중대한 영향) 의 기준을 설명하십시오

sourceInfo.name: CDP Full Corporate Questionnaire and Reporting Guidance 2026
sourceInfo.nameKo: CDP 2026 전체 기업 문항 및 보고 가이던스

sourceInfo.name: CDP Climate Change Scoring Essential Criteria 2026

moduleTitleKo: 의존성, 영향, 리스크 및 기회의 식별, 평가 및 관리
issueTagsKo: 수자원, 해양, 생물다양성, 플라스틱, 기후 변경, 산림
sectorTagsKo: 금융 서비스, 석유 및 가스, 석탄, 일반, 항공, 자본재, 운송 OEM - EPM, 화학, 금속 및 광업, 운송 서비스, 철강, 농산물 원자재, 운송 OEM, 에너지 유틸리티 및 발전, 생물다양성 - 광산, 시멘트, 식품, 음료 & 담배, 제지 및 임업, 부동산, 건설
simpleHelpKo: 이 문항은 '귀사가 정의하는 중대한 영향 (중대한 영향) 의 기준을 설명하십시오'에 대한 배경 설명입니다. CDP 응답자는 보고연도 기준으로 조직의 환경 관련 현황, 리스크, 기회, 성과 또는 관리체계를 문항의 응답 구조에 맞춰 설명해야 합니다.
translationSourceKo: CDP 국문 양식 기반 국문 보정
